# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

○ long-running-harness-9yc [EPIC] · E12: Modulation Matrix   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Nathan Delacrétaz · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-28 · Updated: 2026-03-28

DESCRIPTION
Full source-to-destination modulation routing system for the synthesizer. Source registry: LFO1, LFO2, Amp Envelope, Filter Envelope, Velocity, Aftertouch, Mod Wheel, Pitch Bend. Destination registry: all synth AudioParams (osc1 pitch, osc2 pitch, osc mix, filter cutoff, filter resonance, amp level, LFO1 rate, LFO2 rate). Per-connection: amount control (-100% to +100%), bipolar/unipolar mode. Drag-to-connect UI: source list on left, destination list on right, drag cable visualization. Active routing visualization (colored cables between connected sources and destinations). Real-time modulation application: source value * amount -> added to destination AudioParam. Integration with voice architecture: per-voice modulation for velocity/envelopes, global for LFOs. Modulation matrix panel in synth editor. Serializable for presets and session. EARS: R-OPT-04. Interface exports: ModulationMatrix, ModRoute, ModSource, ModDestination. QA: modulation depth correctness, per-voice vs global routing, parameter range clamping.

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ long-running-harness-13h: E9: Synthesizer Engine ● P1

BLOCKS
  ← ○ long-running-harness-tnf: (EPIC) E18: Integration Verification ● P1

2	 * Shared types for the synthesizer engine.
3	 * Used by both the AudioWorklet processor and main thread.

9	import type { FilterType } from "./dsp/biquad-coeffs";

11	
12	// ─── Voice Commands (main thread → worklet) ───
13	
14	export type SynthVoiceCommand =
15	  | { type: "noteOn"; note: number; velocity: number; legato: boolean }
16	  | { type: "noteOff"; note: number }
17	  | { type: "allNotesOff" }
18	  | { type: "setParam"; key: string; value: number };
19	
20	// ─── Parameter Map ───
21	
22	export type SynthParameterMap = {
23	  // Oscillator 1
24	  osc1Type: WaveformType;
25	  osc1Octave: number; // -2..+2
26	  osc1Detune: number; // cents, -100..+100
27	  osc1Level: number; // 0..1
28

Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a final comprehensive summary:

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

**Project Type:** Web-based Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) built with modern web technologies

**Tech Stack:**
- **Framework:** React 19.2.4 + TypeScript 5.8.3
- **State Management:** Zustand 5.0.12
- **Build Tool:** Vite 8.0.2 with React plugin
- **Audio API:** Web Audio API with AudioWorklet
- **Data Validation:** Zod 4.3.6
- **Testing:** Vitest + React Testing Library
- **Code Quality:** ESLint, Prettier, Husky pre-commit hooks

**Node Path Aliases:**
- `@audio` → `/src/audio`
- `@ui` → `/src/ui`
- `@state` → `/src/state`
- `@` → `/src`

---

### 2. PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
src/
├── audio/                    # Audio engine (Web Audio API + AudioWorklet)
│   ├── synth/               # Synthesizer engine
│   │   ├── dsp/             # DSP components (ADSR, LFO, oscillators, filters)
│   │   ├── synth-types.ts   # Type definitions & MIDI conversion
│   │   ├── synth-instrument.ts  # Main-thread synth controller
│   │   └── synth-processor.ts   # AudioWorklet processor
│   ├── mixer/               # Mixer engine with effects routing
│   ├── effects/             # Effect plugins (Chorus, Delay, Distortion, EQ, etc.)
│   ├── drum-machine/        # Drum sequencer
│   ├── media-pool/          # Audio file decoding & storage
│   ├── midi/                # MIDI device integration
│   ├── engine-context.ts    # AudioContext lifecycle
│   ├── transport-clock.ts   # Playback timing
│   ├── tempo-map.ts         # Tempo/time signature management
│   ├── look-ahead-scheduler.ts  # Event scheduling
│   └── metronome.ts         # Click track
├── state/                    # Zustand stores + session management
│   ├── store.ts             # Main DawStore (transport, tracks, clips)
│   ├── synth/               # Synth parameter store
│   ├── track/               # Track/clip management
│   ├── effects/             # Effects rack state
│   ├── session/             # Session persistence (IDB, auto-save, undo/redo)
│   └── undo/                # Undo/redo command system
├── ui/                       # React components
│   ├── synth/               # SynthEditor.tsx + VirtualKeyboard.tsx
│   ├── mixer/               # MixerPanel, ChannelStrip, MasterStrip
│   ├── effects/             # EffectPanel, EffectsRack
│   ├── piano-roll/          # MIDI editor
│   ├── arrangement/         # Timeline view
│   ├── transport/           # Playback controls
│   ├── controls/            # UI primitives (Fader, Knob, DrumPad, etc.)
│   ├── primitives/          # Base components (Button, Modal, etc.)
│   ├── tokens/              # Design system tokens
│   └── keyboard/            # Keyboard shortcuts
├── App.tsx                   # Root component
└── main.tsx                  # Entry point
```

---

### 3. SYNTHESIZER ENGINE ARCHITECTURE

#### **Core Files:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/src/audio/synth/synth-types.ts` | Type definitions & MIDI conversion |
| `/src/audio/synth/synth-instrument.ts` | Main-thread synth API |
| `/src/audio/synth/synth-processor.ts` | AudioWorklet processor (real-time DSP) |
| `/src/state/synth/synth-store.ts` | Per-track synth parameter state (Zustand) |

#### **DSP Components:**

| File | Type | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `dsp/polyblep.ts` | `PolyBLEPOsc` | Antialiased oscillator (sine, saw, square, triangle) |
| `dsp/adsr.ts` | `ADSREnvelope` | Amplitude & filter envelopes with exponential curves |
| `dsp/lfo.ts` | `LFO` | Modulation oscillators (sine, square, triangle, S&H) |
| `dsp/biquad-coeffs.ts` | `BiquadFilter` | Filter with LP/HP/BP modes |
| `dsp/voice-allocator.ts` | `VoiceAllocator` | 16-voice pool with oldest-steal + 5ms crossfade |

#### **Key Architecture Details:**

**Voice Architecture (16 fixed voices):**
```
Per Voice:
- 2 PolyBLEP oscillators (Osc1, Osc2)
- Biquad filter (LP/HP/BP)
- ADSR amplitude envelope
- ADSR filter envelope
- Pitch glide (portamento)

Shared:
- 2 LFOs (hardwired modulation)
- Voice allocator (oldest-steal algorithm)
```

**Parameter Map** (`SynthParameterMap`):
```typescript
// Oscillators (x2)
- osc{1,2}Type: "sine" | "saw" | "square" | "triangle"
- osc{1,2}Octave: -2..+2
- osc{1,2}Detune: -100..+100 cents
- osc{1,2}Level: 0..1

// Filter
- filterType: "lowpass" | "highpass" | "bandpass"
- filterCutoff: 20..20000 Hz
- filterResonance: 0.5..20 Q

// Amplitude Envelope
- ampAttack, ampDecay, ampSustain (0..1), ampRelease: seconds

// Filter Envelope
- filterAttack, filterDecay, filterSustain (0..1), filterRelease: seconds
- filterEnvDepth: -60..+60 semitones

// LFO 1 & 2
- lfo{1,2}Rate: 0.1..20 Hz
- lfo{1,2}Depth: 0..1
- lfo{1,2}Shape: "sine" | "square" | "triangle" | "sample-and-hold"

// Glide
- glideTime: 0..1 seconds (pitch portamento)

// Master
- masterGain: 0..1
```

#### **Voice Commands** (Main → AudioWorklet):
```typescript
type SynthVoiceCommand =
  | { type: "noteOn"; note: number; velocity: number; legato: boolean }
  | { type: "noteOff"; note: number }
  | { type: "allNotesOff" }
  | { type: "setParam"; key: string; value: number }
```

---

### 4. EXISTING MODULATION SYSTEM

The synth already has a **hardwired modulation matrix** in `synth-processor.ts`:

**Current Modulation Routing:**
1. **LFO1 → Pitch** (in semitones, ±2 semitones max)
2. **LFO1 → Filter Cutoff** (±12 semitones)
3. **Filter Envelope → Filter Cutoff** (±filterEnvDepth)
4. **Velocity → Filter Cutoff** (±24 semitones)
5. **LFO2 → Amplitude** (tremolo, ±50% modulation)

**Modulation Processing:**
```typescript
// Pitch modulation (line 308-310)
const pitchMod = lfo1Val * p.lfo1Depth * 2;
const pitchMultiplier = Math.pow(2, pitchMod / 12);

// Filter cutoff modulation (line 325-330)
const envCutoffMod = filterEnvVal * p.filterEnvDepth;
const lfoCutoffMod = lfo1Val * p.lfo1Depth * 12;
const velCutoffMod = vd.velocity * 24;
const totalCutoffMod = envCutoffMod + lfoCutoffMod + velCutoffMod;
const modulatedCutoff = p.filterCutoff * Math.pow(2, totalCutoffMod / 12);

// Amplitude modulation (line 350)
const ampMod = 1 + lfo2Val * p.lfo2Depth * 0.5;
```

---

### 5. STATE MANAGEMENT PATTERNS

#### **Zustand Stores:**

| Store | File | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `DawStore` | `state/store.ts` | Main app state (transport, tracks, clips, selection) |
| `SynthStore` | `state/synth/synth-store.ts` | Per-track synth parameters |
| `EffectsStore` | `state/effects/effects-store.ts` | Effects rack configuration |
| `MidiStore` | Derived from DawStore | MIDI clip & note event management |

#### **Key Store Methods:**

**DawStore** (Zustand):
```typescript
// Transport
play() | pause() | stop() | setBpm(bpm) | setCursor(seconds)

// Tracks & Clips
addTrack(track) | removeTrack(id) | updateTrack(id, patch)
addClip(clip) | removeClip(id) | moveClip(id, startTime)
addNoteEvent(clipId, note) | removeNoteEvent(clipId, noteId)

// Selection
setSelectedTrackIds(ids) | setSelectedClipIds(ids) | setSelectedNoteIds(ids)

// Query
queryClipsAtTime(time): ClipModel[]
```

**SynthStore** (Zustand):
```typescript
initSynth(trackId)  // Initialize synth state for track
setParam<K>(trackId, key, value)  // Set parameter (synced to AudioWorklet)
setLegato(trackId, enabled)  // Toggle legato mode
getParams(trackId): SynthParameterMap  // Get synth params (or defaults)
```

#### **Session Persistence:**
- `state/session/session-storage.ts` — IDB + in-memory backends
- `state/session/session-schema.ts` — Serialization schema (Zod)
- `state/session/auto-save.ts` — Debounced auto-save to IDB
- `state/session/session-recovery.ts` — Crash recovery

#### **Undo/Redo:**
- `state/undo/undo-manager.ts` — Command-based undo/redo
- `state/undo/undo-command-registry.ts` — Command factory

---

### 6. MIXER & AUDIO ROUTING

#### **Mixer Types** (`src/audio/mixer/types.ts`):

```typescript
type ChannelStrip = {
  trackId: string
  inputGain: GainNode          // Pre-insert input
  inserts: InsertSlot[]         // Effect insert chain
  faderGain: GainNode           // Post-insert fader (logarithmic taper)
  panner: StereoPannerNode      // Stereo pan
  muteGain: GainNode            // Mute control
  analyser: AnalyserNode        // Metering
  muted: boolean
  solo: boolean
  soloIsolate: boolean
}

type MasterBus = {
  inputGain: GainNode
  faderGain: GainNode
  limiter: DynamicsCompressorNode  // Brickwall: -1dBFS, 20:1 ratio
  analyser: AnalyserNode
}
```

#### **Mixer Engine** (`src/audio/mixer/mixer-engine.ts`):
- `getOrCreateStrip(trackId)` — Lazy channel strip creation
- `setFaderLevel(trackId, level)` — Logarithmic taper applied
- `setPan(trackId, pan)` — Stereo panning (-1..1)
- `setMute(trackId, muted)` — Mute/unmute track
- `setSolo(trackId, solo)` — Solo-in-place logic
- `addInsert(trackId, id, input, output)` — Insert effects
- `removeInsert(trackId, insertId)` — Remove effect insert

---

### 7. UI COMPONENT PATTERNS

#### **React + Zustand Pattern:**

**SynthEditor.tsx** example:
```typescript
function SynthEditor({ trackId }: Props) {
  const params = useSynthStore((s) => s.getParams(trackId));
  const setParam = useSynthStore((s) => s.setParam);
  
  const handleOscTypeChange = useCallback((value: WaveformType) => {
    setParam(trackId, "osc1Type", value);
  }, [trackId, setParam]);
  
  // Synth parameters are synced to AudioWorklet via MessagePort
}
```

**Design System** (`src/ui/tokens/tokens.ts`):
- Color palette (gray, primary, secondary)
- Typography (mono, sans, serif)
- Spacing scale
- Border widths
- Neo-brutalist aesthetic

#### **Control Components:**
- `Fader.tsx` — Vertical slider control
- `RotaryKnob.tsx` — Rotary parameter control
- `StepButton.tsx` — MIDI trigger button
- `DrumPad.tsx` — Drum machine pad
- `VuMeter.tsx` — Level metering display
- `Select.tsx`, `Toggle.tsx`, `Modal.tsx` — Primitives

---

### 8. AUDIO PARAMETER MANAGEMENT

#### **MessagePort Communication** (Main Thread ↔ AudioWorklet):

**Main Thread** (`synth-instrument.ts`):
```typescript
// Update local params and send to worklet
setParam<K extends keyof SynthParameterMap>(key: K, value: SynthParameterMap[K]) {
  params[key] = value;
  
  // Convert enums to numeric indices for worklet
  let numericValue = typeof value === "string" 
    ? WAVEFORM_TYPES.indexOf(value)
    : value;
  
  node.port.postMessage({
    type: "setParam",
    key: key as string,
    value: numericValue
  });
}
```

**AudioWorklet** (`synth-processor.ts`):
```typescript
private setParamValue(key: string, value: number): void {
  switch (key) {
    case "osc1Type":
      this.params.osc1Type = WAVEFORM_TYPES[Math.round(value)] ?? "saw";
      break;
    // ... other parameters
  }
}
```

#### **Real-Time Constraints:**
- Zero allocations in `AudioWorkletProcessor.process()`
- Pre-allocated buffers for all DSP state
- Control-rate updates (once per 128-sample quantum)
- Parameter updates via non-blocking MessagePort

---

### 9. TYPE SYSTEM

#### **Key Type Definitions:**

**Audio Types:**
- `SynthParameterMap` — All synth parameters
- `SynthVoiceCommand` — Note on/off, parameter changes
- `SynthInstrument` — Public synth API
- `PolyBLEPOsc` — Oscillator state
- `ADSREnvelope` — Envelope state
- `LFO` — Modulation oscillator state
- `BiquadFilter` — Filter state
- `Voice` — Per-voice state in allocator
- `VoiceAllocator` — 16-voice pool manager

**State Types:**
- `DawStore` — Main application state
- `SynthTrackState` — Per-track synth parameters
- `TrackModel` — Track metadata

**Voice Architecture (16 fixed voices):**
```
Per Voice:
- 2 PolyBLEP oscillators (Osc1, Osc2)
- Biquad filter (LP/HP/BP)
- ADSR amplitude envelope
- ADSR filter envelope
- Pitch glide (portamento)

- filterResonance: 0.5..20 Q

// Amplitude Envelope
- ampAttack, ampDecay, ampSustain (0..1), ampRelease: seconds

// Filter Envelope
- filterAttack, filterDecay, filterSustain (0..1), filterRelease: seconds
- filterEnvDepth: -60..+60 semitones

// LFO 1 & 2
- lfo{1,2}Rate: 0.1..20 Hz
- lfo{1,2}Depth: 0..1
- lfo{1,2}Shape: "sine" | "square" | "triangle" | "sample-and-hold"

```

### 4. EXISTING MODULATION SYSTEM

The synth already has a **hardwired modulation matrix** in `synth-processor.ts`:

**Current Modulation Routing:**
1. **LFO1 → Pitch** (in semitones, ±2 semitones max)
2. **LFO1 → Filter Cutoff** (±12 semitones)
3. **Filter Envelope → Filter Cutoff** (±filterEnvDepth)
4. **Velocity → Filter Cutoff** (±24 semitones)
5. **LFO2 → Amplitude** (tremolo, ±50% modulation)

**Modulation Processing:**
```typescript
// Pitch modulation (line 308-310)
const pitchMod = lfo1Val * p.lfo1Depth * 2;
const pitchMultiplier = Math.pow(2, pitchMod / 12);

// Filter cutoff modulation (line 325-330)
const envCutoffMod = filterEnvVal * p.filterEnvDepth;
const lfoCutoffMod = lfo1Val * p.lfo1Depth * 12;
const velCutoffMod = vd.velocity * 24;
const totalCutoffMod = envCutoffMod + lfoCutoffMod + velCutoffMod;
const modulatedCutoff = p.filterCutoff * Math.pow(2, totalCutoffMod / 12);

// Amplitude modulation (line 350)
const ampMod = 1 + lfo2Val * p.lfo2Depth * 0.5;
```

**Main Thread** (`synth-instrument.ts`):
```typescript
// Update local params and send to worklet
setParam<K extends keyof SynthParameterMap>(key: K, value: SynthParameterMap[K]) {
  params[key] = value;
  
  // Convert enums to numeric indices for worklet
  let numericValue = typeof value === "string" 
    ? WAVEFORM_TYPES.indexOf(value)
    : value;
  
  node.port.postMessage({
    type: "setParam",
    key: key as string,
    value: numericValue
  });
}
```

**AudioWorklet** (`synth-processor.ts`):
```typescript
private setParamValue(key: string, value: number): void {
  switch (key) {
    case "osc1Type":
      this.params.osc1Type = WAVEFORM_TYPES[Math.round(value)] ?? "saw";
      break;
    // ... other parameters
  }
}
```

Now I have a thorough understanding. Let me plan and then implement the modulation matrix. Let me break this into clear tasks:

## Plan

The epic requires:
1. **Types/Registry** - ModSource, ModDestination, ModRoute, ModulationMatrix types
2. **DSP Engine** - Modulation routing in the AudioWorklet processor (replace hardwired modulation)
3. **State Store** - Zustand store for modulation matrix routes per track
4. **Session Serialization** - Add modulation routes to session schema
5. **UI Component** - ModulationMatrix panel with drag-to-connect visualization


6. **Modulation Sources** - I'm defining eight sources: the two LFOs provide continuous global signals, the amp and filter envelopes give per-voice modulation, velocity and aftertouch/mod wheel/pitch bend handle MIDI input
7. **Modulation Destinations** - The oscillator pitches and mix control are the primary targets, with pitch accepting semitone offsets and the full range of modulation signals
8. **Connection Parameters** - Each route has an amount from -1 to +1 to scale the modulation, and a bipolar flag to determine whether the source outputs 0..1 or -1..1
9. **Worklet Architecture** - The modulation matrix lives in the Zustand store and gets sent to the audio worklet as a flat array for efficient zero-allocation processing, replacing the current hardwired modulation with configurable default routes
10. **UI Implementation** - The interface will use drag interactions to configure the modulation matrix with SVG cable visualization

Now I need to think through the per-voice versus global source distinction carefully. Per-voice sources like velocity and envelopes apply independently to each voice, while global sources like the LFOs and mod wheel affect all voices uniformly. When a per-voice source targets a global parameter, I'll use the most recent voice's value for simplicity. The modulation computation in the worklet multiplies each source value by its route amount, keeping per-voice and global modulation separate in their application.

For the implementation, I'll create a flat ModRouteWorklet type with pre-allocated arrays, send routes via a new message type, and iterate through them in the process loop to accumulate modulation offsets per destination. I also need to extend the voice command types to handle MIDI CC sources like aftertouch, mod wheel, and pitch bend, which currently aren't represented in the SynthVoiceCommand structure.

Now I'm planning the TDD approach, starting with types and a registry system, then building out the modulation logic with tests driving the implementation. I need to keep this implementation lean and focused on the core requirements. The epic specifies exact exports—ModulationMatrix, ModRoute, ModSource, ModDestination—so I'll design around those. I'm thinking simple arrays for routes rather than complex graph structures, flat numeric data for the worklet to avoid allocations, and a 32-route limit as a practical ceiling. The UI should be a panel within the synth editor, and I'll start with TDD to keep things disciplined.

For the interface itself, I'm picturing a two-column layout with sources on the left and destinations on the right, where dragging creates visual cable connections with distinct colors per source. Each route gets an amount control. I'll tackle this systematically: first the core types and engine with tests, then the store, integrate it into the processor, build the UI, and finally update the session schema.

Let me fix the `getRoutes` selector in the component - instead of calling `getRoutes(trackId)`, I should access the routes array directly from the store state.

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Modulation matrix types and registries.
3	 * Defines sources, destinations, and routes for the modulation system.
4	 *
5	 * Exports: ModSource, ModDestination, ModRoute, ModulationMatrix
6	 */
7	
8	// ─── Source Registry ───
9	
10	export const MOD_SOURCES = [
11	  "lfo1",
12	  "lfo2",
13	  "ampEnv",
14	  "filterEnv",
15	  "velocity",
16	  "aftertouch",
17	  "modWheel",
18	  "pitchBend",
19	] as const;
20	
21	export type ModSource = (typeof MOD_SOURCES)[number];
22	
23	/** Per-voice sources have a different value per active voice */
24	const PER_VOICE_SOURCES: ReadonlySet<ModSource> = new Set([
25	  "velocity",
26	  "ampEnv",
27	  "filterEnv",
28	]);
29	
30	export function isPerVoiceSource(source: ModSource): boolean {
31	  return PER_VOICE_SOURCES.has(source);
32	}
33	
34	// ─── Destination Registry ───
35	
36	export const MOD_DESTINATIONS = [
37	  "osc1Pitch",
38	  "osc2Pitch",
39	  "oscMix",
40	  "filterCutoff",
41	  "filterResonance",
42	  "ampLevel",
43	  "lfo1Rate",
44	  "lfo2Rate",
45	] as const;
46	
47	export type ModDestination = (typeof MOD_DESTINATIONS)[number];
48	
49	// ─── Route ───
50	
51	export type ModRoute = {
52	  readonly id: string;
53	  readonly source: ModSource;
54	  readonly destination: ModDestination;
55	  readonly amount: number; // -1..+1
56	  readonly bipolar: boolean;
57	};
58	
59	let routeCounter = 0;
60	
61	export function createModRoute(
62	  source: ModSource,
63	  destination: ModDestination,
64	  amount = 0,
65	  bipolar = true,
66	): ModRoute {
67	  return {
68	    id: `mod-${String(++routeCounter)}-${String(Date.now())}`,
69	    source,
70	    destination,
71	    amount: Math.max(-1, Math.min(1, amount)),
72	    bipolar,
73	  };
74	}
75	
76	// ─── Matrix (collection of routes) ───
77	
78	export type ModulationMatrix = {
79	  readonly routes: readonly ModRoute[];
80	};
81	
82	export const EMPTY_MATRIX: ModulationMatrix = { routes: [] };
83	
84	/** Max number of simultaneous routes */
85	export const MAX_MOD_ROUTES = 32;
86

1	/**
2	 * Zod schema for modulation matrix serialization.
3	 * Used for session persistence and preset import/export.
4	 */
5	
6	import { z } from "zod/v4";
7	import { MOD_SOURCES, MOD_DESTINATIONS } from "@audio/synth/modulation-types";
8	
9	export const modulationRouteSchema = z.object({
10	  id: z.string(),
11	  source: z.enum(MOD_SOURCES),
12	  destination: z.enum(MOD_DESTINATIONS),
13	  amount: z.number().min(-1).max(1),
14	  bipolar: z.boolean(),
15	});
16	
17	export const modulationSectionSchema = z.record(
18	  z.string(),
19	  z.array(modulationRouteSchema),
20	);
21	
22	export type ModulationSection = z.infer<typeof modulationSectionSchema>;
23

1	/**
2	 * Shared types for the synthesizer engine.
3	 * Used by both the AudioWorklet processor and main thread.
4	 *
5	 * Exports: SynthParameterMap, SynthVoiceCommand (as specified by epic).
6	 */
7	
8	import type { WaveformType } from "./dsp/polyblep";
9	import type { FilterType } from "./dsp/biquad-coeffs";
10	import type { LFOShape } from "./dsp/lfo";
11	
12	// ─── Voice Commands (main thread → worklet) ───
13	
14	export type SynthVoiceCommand =
15	  | { type: "noteOn"; note: number; velocity: number; legato: boolean }
16	  | { type: "noteOff"; note: number }
17	  | { type: "allNotesOff" }
18	  | { type: "setParam"; key: string; value: number }
19	  | {
20	      type: "setModRoutes";
21	      routes: {
22	        sourceIdx: number;
23	        destIdx: number;
24	        amount: number;
25	        bipolar: boolean;
26	      }[];
27	    }
28	  | { type: "setModSource"; source: string; value: number };
29	
30	// ─── Parameter Map ───
31	
32	export type SynthParameterMap = {
33	  // Oscillator 1
34	  osc1Type: WaveformType;
35	  osc1Octave: number; // -2..+2
36	  osc1Detune: number; // cents, -100..+100
37	  osc1Level: number; // 0..1
38	
39	  // Oscillator 2
40	  osc2Type: WaveformType;
41	  osc2Octave: number;
42	  osc2Detune: number;
43	  osc2Level: number;
44	
45	  // Filter
46	  filterType: FilterType;
47	  filterCutoff: number; // Hz, 20..20000
48	  filterResonance: number; // Q, 0.5..20
49	
50	  // Amplitude Envelope
51	  ampAttack: number; // seconds
52	  ampDecay: number;
53	  ampSustain: number; // 0..1
54	  ampRelease: number;
55	
56	  // Filter Envelope
57	  filterAttack: number;
58	  filterDecay: number;
59	  filterSustain: number; // 0..1
60	  filterRelease: number;
61	  filterEnvDepth: number; // semitones, -60..+60
62	
63	  // LFO 1
64	  lfo1Rate: number; // Hz, 0.1..20
65	  lfo1Depth: number; // 0..1
66	  lfo1Shape: LFOShape;
67	
68	  // LFO 2
69	  lfo2Rate: number; // Hz, 0.1..20
70	  lfo2Depth: number; // 0..1
71	  lfo2Shape: LFOShape;
72	
73	  // Glide
74	  glideTime: number; // seconds, 0..1
75	
76	  // Master
77	  masterGain: number; // 0..1
78	};
79	
80	/** Default parameter values */
81	export const DEFAULT_SYNTH_PARAMS: SynthParameterMap = {
82	  osc1Type: "saw",
83	  osc1Octave: 0,
84	  osc1Detune: 0,
85	  osc1Level: 0.8,
86	
87	  osc2Type: "saw",
88	  osc2Octave: 0,
89	  osc2Detune: 7, // Slight detune for thickness
90	  osc2Level: 0.5,
91	
92	  filterType: "lowpass",
93	  filterCutoff: 5000,
94	  filterResonance: 1,
95	
96	  ampAttack: 0.01,
97	  ampDecay: 0.2,
98	  ampSustain: 0.7,
99	  ampRelease: 0.3,

# Modulation Matrix Code Review

## Summary

The implementation is well-structured overall: clean separation between types, engine, store, schema, and UI. The DSP code follows existing patterns, the Zustand store mirrors `synth-store.ts` conventions, and the Zod schema integrates into the session schema correctly. However, there is one critical zero-allocation violation, several correctness issues, and important missing functionality relative to the epic requirements.

---

## MUST FIX

### 1. Zero-Allocation Violation: `new Float64Array(8)` inside `process()`

**File:** `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/audio/synth/synth-processor.ts`, line 302

```typescript
const voiceSrcValues = new Float64Array(8);
```

This allocates a new `Float64Array` on every call to `process()`, which runs 375 times per second at 48kHz/128-sample blocks. This directly violates the INV-3/NFR-14 zero-allocation constraint documented in the file header. Each allocation creates GC pressure that can cause audio dropouts.

**Fix:** Move `voiceSrcValues` to a pre-allocated class field alongside `modSourceValues` (line 115). The fix is a one-line field declaration plus removing the local declaration -- minimal change.

### 2. Session Persistence Never Serializes or Hydrates Modulation

**File:** `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/state/session/use-session-persistence.ts`

The `storeToSession()` function (line 17) builds the session object but never includes the `modulation` field. The `hydrateStore()` function (line 54) never restores modulation state from `session.modulation`. The schema field exists (`session-schema.ts` line 37) but is dead -- modulation routes are lost on save/reload.

**Fix:** In `storeToSession()`, read from `useModulationStore.getState()` and serialize `matrices` into the session `modulation` field. In `hydrateStore()`, read `session.modulation` and call `useModulationStore.setState()`. Also subscribe `useModulationStore` changes to auto-save (line 145). This follows the exact pattern already established for `useEffectsStore`.

### 3. No Wiring Between Store Changes and Worklet

There is no code anywhere that calls `instrument.setModRoutes()` when the modulation store changes. The store has `getWorkletRoutes()`, the instrument has `setModRoutes()`, but nothing connects them. Routes added in the UI never reach the AudioWorklet processor.

**Fix:** Add a `useEffect` or store subscription (likely in the component that manages the synth instrument lifecycle) that calls `instrument.setModRoutes(getWorkletRoutes(trackId))` whenever the store's routes change. This is the same pattern used for synth parameter changes.

---

## SHOULD IMPROVE

### 4. `routeCounter` Module-Level Mutable State is Not Reset-Safe

**File:** `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/audio/synth/modulation-types.ts`, line 59

```typescript
let routeCounter = 0;
```

This module-level counter combined with `Date.now()` (line 68) works for uniqueness but creates non-deterministic IDs that break snapshot testing and make debugging harder. In test environments where multiple test suites run in the same process, the counter monotonically increases across tests.

**Fix:** Use a crypto-based UUID or at minimum `crypto.randomUUID()` if available, or accept the counter but document the test implications. The simplest fix: keep the counter but drop the `Date.now()` component -- the counter alone guarantees uniqueness within a session.

### 5. LFO Depth Parameters Are Orphaned

**Files:** `synth-types.ts` lines 65, 70 define `lfo1Depth` and `lfo2Depth`. `synth-processor.ts` never reads them.

With the old hardwired modulation replaced by the route-based system, these parameters have no effect. The LFO raw output (range [-1, 1]) feeds directly into the modulation matrix without depth scaling. Users adjusting "LFO Depth" knobs in the SynthEditor will see no effect.

**Fix:** Either (a) scale LFO output by depth before writing to `modSourceValues` in the processor (e.g., `this.modSourceValues[SOURCE_INDEX.lfo1] = lfo1Val * p.lfo1Depth`), or (b) deprecate/remove the depth parameters and let route amounts serve as the depth control. Option (b) is simpler and avoids redundant controls, but is a UX decision.

### 6. SVG Cable Visualization Uses Percentage x2 in `<line>`

**File:** `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/synth/ModulationMatrix.tsx`, line 203

```tsx
x2="100%"
```

SVG `<line>` coordinates do not support percentage values in all rendering contexts. While modern browsers handle it, it is technically non-standard for `<line>` attributes (percentages are defined for some SVG elements but not `x1/y1/x2/y2` on `<line>` in SVG 1.1). The y-coordinates use magic numbers (`20 + idx * 22`) that will drift from actual port positions if the layout changes.

**Fix:** Use `useRef` on port elements and calculate actual positions via `getBoundingClientRect()` relative to the SVG container, or switch to a viewBox-based coordinate system where the width is known.

### 7. No Bipolar Toggle in the UI

The epic requires "per-connection bipolar/unipolar mode," but the `ModulationMatrix` UI component has no toggle for the `bipolar` field. Routes are always created with `bipolar: true` (default). Users cannot change a route to unipolar mode.

**Fix:** Add a small toggle button next to each route's amount slider in the routes list. The store's `updateRouteAmount` pattern can be replicated as an `updateRouteBipolar` action.

---

## COULD ENHANCE

### 8. Store Missing `updateRouteBipolar` Action

**File:** `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/state/synth/modulation-store.ts`

The store has `updateRouteAmount` but no `updateRouteBipolar`. This is needed for the bipolar toggle UI.

**Fix:** Add an action following the exact same pattern as `updateRouteAmount`, mapping `r.id === routeId ? { ...r, bipolar } : r`.

### 9. `getRoutes` and `getWorkletRoutes` Are Derived State as Store Actions

**File:** `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/state/synth/modulation-store.ts`, lines 118-130

The `getRoutes` and `getWorkletRoutes` methods use `get()` (synchronous reads) rather than being selectors. This is functional but inconsistent with React best practices for Zustand -- consumers should use selectors for reactivity. `getWorkletRoutes` in particular creates a new array on every call (`.map()`), which is fine for imperative use but would cause infinite re-renders if used in a React component selector.

**Fix:** Keep `getWorkletRoutes` as an imperative method (it is only called from instrument wiring code). Document that it is not a React selector.

### 10. ModulationMatrix Component Does Not Handle Missing Matrix Gracefully

**File:** `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/synth/ModulationMatrix.tsx`, line 81

The component reads `s.matrices[trackId]?.routes ?? EMPTY_ROUTES` which is correct, but `SynthEditor` renders `<ModulationMatrix trackId={trackId} />` without ensuring `initMatrix` has been called. If the matrix is never initialized, the add-route drag-to-connect silently fails (because `addRoute` checks `if (!matrix) return s`).

**Fix:** Call `initMatrix(trackId)` in a `useEffect` within `ModulationMatrix`, or ensure it is called alongside `initSynth(trackId)` in the track lifecycle.

### 11. Test Coverage Gaps

- No test for the `setModRoutes` and `setModSource` message handling in the processor (integration level).
- No test that verifies `voiceSrcValues` are correctly populated per-voice (the per-voice modulation path).
- The UI test does not verify the cancel-drag behavior (mouseUp on the container without hitting a destination).
- No test for the session round-trip (serialize modulation routes, hydrate them back).

---

## Prioritized Action Plan

| Priority | Issue | Impact | Effort |
|----------|-------|--------|--------|
| P0 | #1 - Move `voiceSrcValues` to pre-allocated field | Prevents audio dropouts | 2 lines |
| P0 | #3 - Wire store changes to worklet via subscription | Makes feature functional | ~15 lines |
| P0 | #2 - Serialize/hydrate modulation in session persistence | Prevents data loss | ~20 lines |
| P1 | #5 - Reconcile LFO depth with modulation matrix | Fixes dead UI controls | 2 lines (option a) or remove knobs |
| P1 | #7 + #8 - Add bipolar toggle UI + store action | Completes epic requirement | ~20 lines |
| P2 | #10 - Auto-init matrix in component or lifecycle | Prevents silent failures | 3 lines |
| P2 | #4 - Simplify route ID generation | Improves testability | 1 line |
| P3 | #6 - Fix SVG cable coordinate system | Visual correctness | ~15 lines |
| P3 | #9 - Document `getWorkletRoutes` as non-reactive | Clarifies intent | 1 comment |
| P3 | #11 - Add missing tests | Coverage | ~50 lines |

The three P0 items are blocking: without them, the modulation matrix is visually present but (a) allocates in the audio thread, (b) never actually modulates sound, and (c) loses state on reload. Fix those first, then address the P1 items to complete the epic's feature requirements.

**Fix:** Call `initMatrix(trackId)` in a `useEffect` within `ModulationMatrix`, or ensure it is called alongside `initSynth(trackId)` in the track lifecycle.

### 11. Test Coverage Gaps

- No test for the `setModRoutes` and `setModSource` message handling in the processor (integration level).
- No test that verifies `voiceSrcValues` are correctly populated per-voice (the per-voice modulation path).
- The UI test does not verify the cancel-drag behavior (mouseUp on the container without hitting a destination).
- No test for the session round-trip (serialize modulation routes, hydrate them back).
